_meta.author: ChrisPachulski
notes: plex:   plex:(0|[1-9][0-9]*) — no leading zeros; plex:0 is valid (single zero); plex:007 is INVALID.
local:  local:[0-9A-HJKMNP-TV-Z]{26} — Crockford Base32 ULID, exactly 26 chars, uppercase only.
parseSub is provider-dispatching (not a single regex) per §8.3 pseudocode.
parseSub trims leading/trailing whitespace before validation and throws sub_invalid_format if trimming was required (not silent pass-through).
These 13 vectors are the ground truth for TS parseSub (D7), Rust emerald-contracts::sub::parse_sub (M2+), and Swift EmeraldKit/Sub.parseSub (M2+).

vectors.name: valid-plex-zero
vectors.note: Single zero is the only valid single-digit plex id. Pattern plex:(0|[1-9][0-9]*) explicitly allows this.

vectors.name: valid-plex-integer
vectors.note: Standard positive plex id, no leading zeros.

vectors.name: valid-local-ulid
vectors.note: 26-char Crockford Base32 ULID. Contains 0 and adjacent chars to confirm O is excluded (the charset has no O). All uppercase as required by §8.1.

vectors.name: valid-apple-siwa
vectors.note: Real-world SIWA sub shape per §8.3: [0-9]{6}.[0-9a-f]{32}.[0-9]{4}. Dot chars in sub are not JSON special chars per §5.1 note.

vectors.name: invalid-plex-leading-zero
vectors.note: §8.1 MANDATES rejection of leading zeros: plex:(0|[1-9][0-9]*). plex:007 does not match — 007 starts with 0 but is not a single 0. Rust Sub::Plex(u64) cannot round-trip leading-zero forms through the §5.1 canonical serializer.

vectors.name: invalid-plex-alpha-id
vectors.note: Plex id must be numeric. 'abc' does not match (0|[1-9][0-9]*).

vectors.name: invalid-plex-empty-id
vectors.note: Empty id after colon. Pattern requires at least one digit: (0|[1-9][0-9]*) cannot match empty string.

vectors.name: invalid-local-lowercase
vectors.note: §8.1: local ULIDs are stored and compared uppercase. Lowercase chars are outside [0-9A-HJKMNP-TV-Z]. This input is also 33 chars (too long), compounding the invalidity.

vectors.name: invalid-local-too-short
vectors.note: ULID must be exactly 26 chars. '01HABC' is 6 chars — far too short. [0-9A-HJKMNP-TV-Z]{26} requires exactly 26.

vectors.name: invalid-apple-bad-format
vectors.note: Does not match [0-9]{6}.[0-9a-f]{32}.[0-9]{4}. No dots, no hex segment, wrong length.

vectors.name: invalid-no-provider
vectors.note: No colon present. parseSub returns indexOf(':') == -1 and throws sub_missing_namespace per §8.3 pseudocode.

vectors.name: invalid-empty-prefix
vectors.note: Colon is present but provider prefix is empty string. provider = s.slice(0, 0) = ''. None of the three provider branches match, so throws sub_invalid_format.

vectors.name: invalid-extra-colons
vectors.note: Provider-dispatching parser uses indexOf(':') to split at the FIRST colon only. provider='plex', id='0:extra:colons'. id does not match (0|[1-9][0-9]*) because of the embedded colons, so throws sub_invalid_format.
